html.p: This app renders SPL results as a dynamic form with freetext, dropdown, radio, checkbox, number, date, spl, description, markdown, and spl_display rows. Responses are saved to a global KV Store collection and tagged with form_name for filtering.
Run SPL that returns these columns:

ul.li: question (required): label shown above the form field
type (required): freetext, dropdown, radio, checkbox, number, date, spl (multi-line SPL input with a Format button), description, markdown, or spl_display (read-only auto-formatted SPL)
options: comma-separated choices for dropdown/radio/checkbox; description body text for description rows; the SPL string to render for spl_display rows
order (optional): numeric sort order for rendered fields
description (optional): markdown helper text shown under the question label and above the input (the description_above column is still accepted as a backward-compatible alias)
Form Name and Output Lookup set the KV Store destination metadata
Display Orders optionally renders only selected order numbers/ranges, for example 1,2,3, 1-5, or 5-9
Form Name Token (default form_name)
Output Lookup Token (default form_output_lookup)
If order exists and is numeric, fields are sorted ascending by order
Ties keep original SPL row order
Rows without numeric order render after ordered rows
When Display Orders is set, rows outside that filter and rows without numeric order are hidden from that panel
$$form_response$$: JSON of answers plus _form_name and _form_output_lookup
$$form_name$$ and $$form_output_lookup$$ (token names configurable)

html.p: Every answered question from one Submit shares the same submission_id. It is generated in the browser when the user clicks Submit Form:

The timestamp uses browser Date.getTime(). The form portion is the resolved form name. The order scope is the Display Orders value with spaces removed when a display filter is set; otherwise it is the sorted answered question orders joined with _, or noorder when answered rows do not have numeric order values. The user portion uses $$env:user$$ when Capture User is enabled, otherwise anon. Text parts are normalized to lowercase safe characters. Use it to group one submission batch; each KV Store row still has its own _key.

ul.li: For dynamic outputlookup updates of CSV lookups, keep the visualization and lookup in the same app context. Cross-app outputlookup can create app-local shadow lookup files.
If duplicate question labels are used, token payload keys can collide.
Added Display Orders panel setting for rendering selected order numbers/ranges from a larger form definition
submission_id now uses <epoch-ms>-<form-name>-<order-scope>-<user>
Submitted records include the question order whenever the answered row has one
